title: Autoload Bookmap
author: Consistent-Gas-8677
selftext:
Hey there folks,

So, I've been fiddling around with a neat little project of mine. I'm working on automating the loading of new coin data based on the reactions I get from this clever telegram bot I cobbled together. The goal is to pump this data into Bookmap.

While it's already working-ish, it's still kinda buggy and could use a fine-tuning. Do any of you code wizards out there have some experience with something like this? Maybe you could nudge me in the right direction?

If you're game, slide into my PMs and we can have a good ol' geek out.

P.S. I'm also thinking of putting together a Telegram group for us Trading Bot enthusiasts. We can chat, network, and share some of that sweet, sweet knowledge. If that tickles your fancy, keep an eye on this post.

If we can round up an enthusiastic posse of 50 or more, I'll go ahead and set it up. And of course, I could use a few brave souls to help moderate the group.

Now, don't worry. I'm not asking you to give away all your secret sauce recipes. This is all about sharing and networking. Not a place for lazy cats looking for a free ride ;)"

As for the step-by-step requirements for your automation project, it might look something like this:

1. Develop a Telegram bot that can monitor and react to specific events or triggers (from the screener)
2. Ensure the bot can receive and process the relevant coin data.
3. Set up a data pipeline that can receive data from the Telegram bot and load it into Bookmap.
4. Develop error handling mechanisms to deal with any issues or bugs that might arise during the data transfer process.
5. Create a testing protocol to ensure the system is working properly.
6. Regularly monitor and maintain the system to ensure it's operating optimally.
